name: Direct Lake Semantic Model + Report
description: Create a Direct Lake semantic model from lakehouse Delta tables and bind a report for zero-copy analytics.
prerequisites: Lakehouse with at least one Delta table loaded

steps.command: fabio lakehouse load-table --workspace $WS_ID --id $LH_ID --table sales --source-path Files/staging/sales.csv --mode Overwrite --format Csv --header true

steps.description: Get the SQL endpoint ID (needed for the model connection).
Create Direct Lake semantic model from model.bim with connection to SQL endpoint.
Frame the model (required before DAX queries work). Wait 15-30s after.
Verify model works with a DAX query.
Create report bound to the semantic model.

model_bim_template.description: A minimal model.bim for a Direct Lake semantic model. Replace PLACEHOLDERS with actual values.

key_fields.partition.source.entityName: Must match the Delta table name

model_bim.when: Quick single-table models. Use model.bim (JSON TMSL) with compatibilityLevel 1604.
model_bim.notes: The --connection flag binds the model to the lakehouse SQL endpoint. This is required for Direct Lake.

tmdl.command: fabio semantic-model create --workspace $WS --name NAME --file model.tmdl --file tables/table.tmdl --file definition.pbism --connection $SQL_ENDPOINT_ID
tmdl.when: Multi-table models or complex expressions. TMDL is the recommended format for production models.
tmdl.notes: TMDL separates concerns (one file per table). definition.pbism must use version 4.2 with Fabric schema URL.

tips: model.bim WORKS for Direct Lake — use compatibilityLevel 1604 and defaultMode: directLake.
TMDL is preferred for multi-table models but model.bim is simpler for single-table cases.
The --connection flag on semantic-model create is required — it links to the SQL endpoint ID (not the lakehouse ID).
Get the SQL endpoint ID from: fabio lakehouse show --workspace $WS --id $LH --query 'data.properties.sqlEndpointProperties.id' -o plain
After refresh, wait 15-30 seconds before querying with DAX.
Use fabio lakehouse table-schema to discover column names and types for building the model.bim.
Use --query 'data.id' -o plain to extract created item IDs for chaining commands.
